Understanding GST in India — Your Complete Guide

GST (Goods and Services Tax) — the full form of GST — is India's most significant indirect tax reform. Implemented on 1 July 2017, GST replaced a fragmented system of central and state taxes. Today, every business with annual turnover above the threshold must complete their GST registration on the official GST portal. Once registered, a business receives a unique 15-character GST number. You can do a quick GST number check or GST number search to verify details of any supplier before transacting, ensuring they are active on the GST portal login.

Whether you're a small trader or a seasoned CA, having a reliable GST tool is essential. India uses a four-tier GST rate structure. To figure out tax on a bill, you can use our free GST calculator. It allows you to add GST to a base price or remove GST from an inclusive price in seconds. Understanding new GST rates and applying them correctly ensures correct GST payment when filing returns.

What is a GST Number?

A GST number (also known as GSTIN) is an alphanumeric identifier assigned after successful gst registration. Knowing how to do a GST no check or GST no search is critical before entering a business transaction. Our GST verification tool lets you validate the checksum and PAN immediately without any external API call. You don't need a separate gst login to use our offline tools.

HSN Codes and GST Search

Every good traded in India is classified under an HSN code, and services under a SAC. These codes determine the applicable new gst rates list 2025. Doing a quick GST search through our HSN Lookup tool lets you find the exact code and its associated tax rate. This avoids errors during gst portal login and invoice generation. Our GST status tool can also assist in tracking the correct rates and filing requirements.

GST Portal Login and Payments

The official website is where you perform your gst login portal activities. This is your destination for filing returns, tracking your gst status, and making a secure gst payment. You can also download your certificate or perform a real-time gst check. While the official portal is essential for compliance, our fast, offline tools save you time during day-to-day operations like preparing invoices or performing a quick gst verification before paying vendors.

Note: All tools on this website are for informational and calculation purposes only. GST rates and rules are subject to change. Always refer to the official GST portal and CBIC notifications for authoritative information. Our gst tool suite is completely free and requires no gst login.

Frequently Asked Questions

View all FAQs →
What is GST?

GST (Goods and Services Tax) is a comprehensive, multi-stage, destination-based indirect tax levied on every value addition in India. It replaced a complex web of central and state taxes such as VAT, service tax, excise duty, and CST. GST is collected at each stage of the supply chain, but the tax burden ultimately falls on the final consumer.

How to get GST number?

To get a GST number (GSTIN), follow these steps:

  1. Visit the official GST portal at gst.gov.in.
  2. Click on "Services → Registration → New Registration".
  3. Fill in Part A with your PAN, mobile number, and email.
  4. Verify via OTP and receive a Temporary Reference Number (TRN).
  5. Complete Part B with business details, documents (PAN, Aadhaar, address proof, bank details), and submit.
  6. An ARN (Application Reference Number) is generated.
  7. A GST officer reviews and approves; GSTIN is issued within 7 working days.
GST kab lagu hua?

GST bharat mein 1 July 2017 ko lagu hua. Yeh ek aitihaasik kar sudhar tha jisme "One Nation, One Tax" ka concept implement kiya gaya. GST Council ki pehli meeting 22–23 September 2016 ko hui thi.

How to download GST certificate?

To download your GST Registration Certificate:

  1. Log in to the GST portal at gst.gov.in using your credentials.
  2. Go to Services → User Services → View/Download Certificates.
  3. Select the certificate type (Registration Certificate — Form GST REG-06).
  4. Click "Download" to save the PDF.
How to file GST return?

GST returns are filed online on the GST portal. The main return forms are: GSTR-1 (outward supplies) — monthly or quarterly. GSTR-3B (summary return with tax payment) — monthly. GSTR-9 (annual return) — once a year. Steps: Log in to gst.gov.in → Services → Returns → Returns Dashboard → select period → fill details → pay tax (if any) → submit and file with DSC or EVC.

GST number kya hota hai?

GST number (GSTIN) ek 15-character ka alphanumeric code hota hai jo har GST-registered business ko diya jata hai. Iska format hai: 2-digit state code + 10-digit PAN + 1-digit entity number + "Z" + 1 checksum digit. Jaise: 27AABCU9603R1ZX. Aap hamare GSTIN Validator se koi bhi GST number check kar sakte hain.
